novel_features.name: Cross-Meeting Commitment Tracker
novel_features.description: See every open action item you promised across all calls — grouped by meeting, assignee, and date — without opening a single recording.
novel_features.rationale: Requires cross-meeting action item aggregation across all meetings in a local SQLite store; no API endpoint exists for 'all my open promises across all recordings.'
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when you need a weekly accountability audit of all meeting commitments before a 1:1 or team sync.

novel_features.name: Topic Frequency Tracker
novel_features.description: Find out how often 'pricing,' 'onboarding,' or any keyword has surfaced in your meetings over the past N weeks — with week-over-week trend.
novel_features.rationale: FTS5 term counting over all locally stored transcripts by ISO week; 60 req/min rate limit makes live scanning of 100+ transcripts infeasible without a local store.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use before a board meeting or quarterly review to synthesize what themes have been dominating customer calls.

novel_features.name: Pre-Call Account Brief
novel_features.description: Get a chronological history of every meeting with a specific person or company — past topics, open action items, last contact date — before you join a call.
novel_features.rationale: Participant-keyed retrospective joining meetings, summaries, and action items in local SQLite; no single API endpoint returns all history with a given person or domain.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use immediately before a customer call to surface prior commitments and context without opening multiple browser tabs.

novel_features.name: Engagement Velocity Tracker
novel_features.description: Track whether your meeting cadence with a key account is accelerating, stable, or stalling — month by month.
novel_features.rationale: Month-by-month meeting count per external domain computed from all participant records; live-API tools cannot compute this without exhausting the 60 req/min limit.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use for pipeline health reviews: a stalling cadence with a key account is an early warning signal before the deal goes cold.

novel_features.name: Team Meeting Workload
novel_features.description: See which team members are spending the most hours in meetings per week and whether the load is worsening.
novel_features.rationale: Per-member weekly meeting hour aggregation requires joining every meeting a person appeared in; live scan would exhaust rate limit for a 10-person team in under a minute.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use in 1:1 prep or team planning to identify who is in 'meeting hell' before assigning more collaborative work.

novel_features.name: Account Relationship History
novel_features.description: View a complete, domain-keyed history with any company: every meeting, topics discussed, action items, and cadence — in one structured output.
novel_features.rationale: Fathom's API is recording-centric; account-centric intelligence requires a domain-keyed join across meetings, participants, and action items that only a local store enables.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use during account reviews, renewal prep, or CRM updates to get a full picture of all interactions with a company.

novel_features.name: Stale Recording Detector
novel_features.description: Find recordings that were captured but have no transcript, summary, or action items synced — useful for operators debugging pipeline gaps.
novel_features.rationale: Store integrity introspection differentiates never-synced, empty-transcript, and summary-missing states; live API cannot distinguish 'webhook fired but data missing' from 'never synced.'
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use on Monday morning to audit which recordings from last week are missing data before your team needs them.

novel_features.name: CRM Gap Audit
novel_features.description: Surface CRM-matched meetings where no action items were logged — calls that touched active deals but left no paper trail.
novel_features.rationale: Three-table join across meetings, crm_matches, and action_items; this sales hygiene query is impossible with per-meeting live API calls.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use in RevOps audits to find sales calls where reps talked to prospects but forgot to log next steps in the CRM.

novel_features.name: Recording Coverage Report
novel_features.description: Track how reliably a recurring meeting (weekly planning, standup, 1:1) is being recorded over time.
novel_features.rationale: Coverage detection across a recurring series requires historical local data; the API returns no 'series' concept, so meetings are grouped by fuzzy title pattern.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use to verify that mandatory-record meetings are actually being captured before auditing team performance.

narrative.headline: Sync your Fathom meetings once, then search, analyze, and act on them forever — offline, at scale, without burning API quota.
narrative.value_prop: fathom-pp-cli pulls every meeting, transcript, summary, and action item into a local SQLite store, then unlocks cross-meeting intelligence no MCP server or web UI can provide: commitment tracking across all your calls, topic trend analysis over weeks, pre-call account briefs, pipeline velocity detection, and team meeting-load audits.
narrative.auth_narrative: Fathom uses an API key passed via the X-Api-Key header. Generate one at fathom.video → User Settings → API Access. Set FATHOM_PP_CLI_API_KEY in your environment, then run doctor to verify.

quickstart.comment: Pull all meetings, transcripts, summaries, and action items into local SQLite
See everything you've promised in the last 30 days
Track topic frequency trends across recent customer calls
Get a pre-call brief for your next Acme meeting

troubleshoots.fix: Check FATHOM_PP_CLI_API_KEY is set; generate a new key at fathom.video → User Settings → API Access
Fathom rate limit is 60 req/min; run fathom-pp-cli sync with --throttle to slow down bulk fetching

troubleshoots.symptom: transcript or summary is empty in local store
troubleshoots.fix: Run fathom-pp-cli stale --since 7d to find affected recordings, then fathom-pp-cli sync --recording-id <id> to re-sync individually

troubleshoots.symptom: brief or account shows no results for a domain
troubleshoots.fix: Ensure sync ran with --include-transcript --include-summary --include-action-items; re-run fathom-pp-cli sync --full if needed

narrative.when_to_use: Use fathom-pp-cli when you need cross-meeting intelligence that the Fathom web UI and MCP servers cannot provide: commiment audits across all calls, topic trend analysis over weeks or months, pre-call account briefs, or pipeline cadence monitoring. Run sync once per day to keep the local store current, then all analysis commands run offline. Not a substitute for the Fathom web UI for reviewing individual recordings or managing your account.

recipes.title: Pre-call brief for a customer
recipes.explanation: Pull all history with Acme before joining a call — past topics, open commitments, last meeting date.

recipes.title: Weekly commitment audit
recipes.explanation: Surface every action item from last week's calls, grouped by meeting — know what's still open before Monday standup.

recipes.title: Topic trend for board prep
recipes.explanation: Show week-over-week frequency of key themes across all customer calls for the past quarter.

recipes.title: Pipeline velocity check
recipes.explanation: See whether your meeting cadence with Acme has been increasing, flat, or stalling — early warning for deals going cold.

recipes.title: Team meeting load audit
recipes.explanation: Find which engineers are spending over 15h/week in meetings and whether it's getting worse.

trigger_phrases: what did I promise in my calls
how often has pricing come up in meetings
brief me on my Acme calls
which team members are in too many meetings
